assumptions: The exposure considered is a foreign-currency-denominated intragroup dividend receivable recognised as a monetary item.
The question concerns the consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a top-level hedge accounting model for exposure to variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify a net investment hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation.

approaches.label: IAS 21 foreign currency accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 21 provides the baseline accounting for exchange differences on foreign-currency monetary items, including intragroup monetary balances in consolidated financial statements.
